highlights.text: I wrote Friday at 4pm: “In a strong bull case, ES won’t even re-test 5978.
I concluded yesterdays newsletter by stating: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
To do that, we need a setup, and for me, this means Failed Breakdowns.
Last Thursday evening, the above two points and Failed Breakdown married together nicely.
I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm in preparation that “I especially like the Failed Breakdown of last Thursdays 5981 low especially if we tag 5978 first.” What did we see Thursday evening?
I’d prefer to see a Failed Breakdown of this afternoons lows to long here, ideal would be if it can tag 6026 first.” At 3pm, we hit 6026 *exact* recovered 6034, and rallied to 6045-50 again.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a quality Failed Breakdown we had right off the Sunday open this week at 6028 (provided to readers on Friday) which triggered an 80 point leg up.
Sunday Gap Down Failed Breakdowns are one of my favorite setups.
2) Since June 5th, ES had found itself stuck in a range with 5979 support, 6099 resistance, and a 6050 magnet in between.
Today was a goldmine for level to level traders but ultimately we spent it mostly at the 6050 magnet.

post_title: SPX Is Coiled Ultra Tight *Again* For A Move. What Way? June 19/20 Plan
